questions.question_text: Which protected class is recognized under New Jersey's LAD but NOT under the federal Fair Housing Act?
distractors: Race and color
Familial status
Disability or handicap
questions.explanation: New Jersey's Law Against Discrimination adds several classes beyond federal protections, including source of income. This means landlords cannot refuse Section 8 voucher holders solely because of their payment source.

questions.question_text: A landlord in NJ owns a duplex, lives in one unit, and refuses to rent the other unit based on race. Is this lawful under the LAD?
questions.correct_answer: No, the LAD has no Mrs. Murphy exemption
distractors: Yes, owner-occupied duplexes are fully exempt
Yes, if the landlord does not use a broker
No, but only if the property has five or more units
questions.explanation: Unlike the federal Fair Housing Act, which exempts owner-occupied properties with four or fewer units (Mrs. Murphy exemption), New Jersey's LAD provides NO such exemption. Discrimination is prohibited in virtually all housing.

questions.question_text: A NJ landlord rejects a tenant's application solely because the tenant uses a Section 8 housing voucher. What law does this violate?
distractors: The federal Fair Housing Act only
The NJ Tenant Protection Act of 2020
The Consumer Protection and Equity Act
questions.explanation: New Jersey's LAD protects source of income as a class. Refusing a tenant solely because they use a Section 8 voucher constitutes source-of-income discrimination and violates state law.

questions.question_text: How does the NJ LAD differ from federal fair housing law regarding the scope of housing exemptions?
questions.correct_answer: The LAD applies to virtually all housing
distractors: The LAD exempts single-family home rentals
The LAD only covers public housing units
The LAD mirrors federal exemptions exactly
questions.explanation: The NJ LAD is one of the broadest anti-discrimination laws in the nation. It applies to virtually all housing with very few exemptions, unlike federal law which includes the Mrs. Murphy exemption for owner-occupied small properties.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a protected class under the NJ LAD?
distractors: Occupation or profession
Educational background
Political party affiliation
questions.explanation: The NJ LAD protects gender identity or expression in addition to federal classes. Other NJ-specific classes include creed, ancestry, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, affectional or sexual orientation, and source of income.

questions.question_text: An agent in NJ only shows homes in certain neighborhoods to minority buyers based on their race. What prohibited practice is this?
distractors: Blockbusting
Redlining
Churning
questions.explanation: Steering occurs when an agent directs buyers toward or away from certain neighborhoods based on protected class characteristics. This practice is prohibited under both the NJ LAD and federal fair housing law.

questions.question_text: A property manager places an ad stating 'perfect for young professionals, no children.' Under the NJ LAD, what violation has occurred?
questions.correct_answer: Discriminatory advertising based on familial status
distractors: No violation if the property has one bedroom
A zoning violation but not a fair housing issue
Discrimination only if units are publicly funded
questions.explanation: Advertising that discourages families with children violates both federal and NJ fair housing laws. Familial status is a protected class, and discriminatory advertising is a prohibited practice under the LAD regardless of property size.

questions.question_text: An agent tells homeowners that people of a different ethnic background are moving into the neighborhood to induce panic selling. This practice is called:
distractors: Steering
Redlining
Puffing
questions.explanation: Blockbusting involves inducing homeowners to sell by suggesting that members of a particular protected class are moving into the area. This exploits fear and prejudice and is prohibited under the NJ LAD and federal law.

questions.question_text: A lender in NJ refuses mortgage loans for properties in predominantly minority neighborhoods. What prohibited practice does this describe?
distractors: Steering by the lending institution
Blockbusting through loan denial
Predatory lending practices
questions.explanation: Redlining is the practice of denying or limiting financial services to certain neighborhoods based on racial or ethnic composition. It is prohibited under both the NJ LAD and federal fair lending laws.

questions.question_text: Under the NJ LAD, which of the following housing transactions is subject to anti-discrimination requirements?
questions.correct_answer: Sale, rental, and financing of housing
distractors: Only sales of newly constructed homes
Only rental transactions over six months
Only properties managed by licensed brokers
questions.explanation: The NJ LAD prohibits discrimination across all major housing transactions including sales, rentals, and financing. Its broad scope covers virtually all housing with very few exemptions, making it one of the most comprehensive state fair housing laws.

questions.question_text: Which state agency is responsible for enforcing the NJ Law Against Discrimination?
questions.correct_answer: The NJ Division on Civil Rights
distractors: The NJ Real Estate Commission
The NJ Department of Housing
The NJ Attorney General exclusively
questions.explanation: The NJ Division on Civil Rights is the primary enforcement agency for the LAD. It investigates complaints, conducts hearings, and can impose remedies including damages and injunctive relief.

questions.question_text: A tenant wins a housing discrimination case under the NJ LAD. Which remedies may the court award?
questions.correct_answer: Compensatory damages, punitive damages, and fees
distractors: Only compensatory damages are available
Criminal penalties and jail time only
A written apology and future compliance plan
questions.explanation: The NJ LAD provides robust remedies including compensatory damages, punitive damages, attorney fees, and injunctive relief. This comprehensive remedy structure makes it one of the strongest anti-discrimination laws in the nation.

questions.question_text: A broker repeatedly steers minority clients away from affluent NJ neighborhoods. The Division on Civil Rights investigates. What type of relief can it order?
questions.correct_answer: Injunctive relief to stop the practice
distractors: Only a verbal warning for first offenses
License suspension but no monetary damages
Referral to federal court without state action
questions.explanation: The Division on Civil Rights can order injunctive relief requiring the broker to cease discriminatory practices. Additional remedies may include compensatory damages, punitive damages, and attorney fees for the victims.

questions.question_text: Why is the NJ LAD considered one of the broadest anti-discrimination statutes in the United States?
questions.correct_answer: It covers more classes with fewer exemptions
distractors: It imposes criminal penalties for all violations
It applies only to government-funded housing
It was the first state fair housing law enacted
questions.explanation: The NJ LAD is exceptionally broad because it protects additional classes beyond federal law (such as source of income), has virtually no housing exemptions (no Mrs. Murphy exemption), and provides comprehensive remedies including punitive damages.

questions.question_text: A complainant files a housing discrimination claim with the NJ Division on Civil Rights. What is the initial step in the enforcement process?
distractors: The case goes directly to Superior Court
The respondent must pay a preliminary fine
Mediation is mandatory before any investigation
questions.explanation: When a complaint is filed with the NJ Division on Civil Rights, the agency investigates the allegations to determine if probable cause exists. If found, the case may proceed to a hearing or settlement.
